items.title: If the odds are anything to go by, this 104yo WWII veteran should not be alive

children.content: Eighty-five years ago, Brian Walley was a prisoner of war in a German camp after crashing his bomber into the freezing North Sea, and he doubted he would live for long. Now, he has celebrated his 104th birthday.

items.title: Can the Socceroos beat Egypt?

children.content: Can the Socceroos beat Egypt and win their first ever knockout final at a World Cup?
The Pharaohs have Salah, Marmoush and a dangerous counterattack, but the Socceroos have the physical edge, aerial threats and set-piece power to cause serious problems.
We take a closer look Egypt's strengths, and why Australia are a dangerous underdog that shouldn't be written off.

articles.title: Man accused of Thai girl's murder grew up and owned firearms in WA, moved eight months ago

children.content: Australian Simon Peter Carman went about "normal life", going out, washing his clothes and talking to neighbours after allegedly murdering a teenage girl and stuffing her body inside a suitcase in Thailand, police say.
